market_overview.headline: Markets rose on Iran ceasefire extension hopes despite narrow EMA leadership showing signs of stress.
market_overview.detail: 38% of stocks are Full Bull but both SPY and QQQ show compressed EMA spreads with bearish cross risk, while BTC remains in bear rally mode below 21W despite strong daily gains.

reversal_candidates.headline: Bear Rally Reversal Setups (Strong Crossover Alerts)

items.name: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.
items.note: AI design software leader showing strong momentum with 8W/13W crossover imminent after 11% rally above both EMAs.

items.name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
items.note: Cybersecurity powerhouse bouncing hard with 11% gain above 13W and EMA crossover setup developing.

items.name: Palo Alto Networks, Inc.
items.note: Another cybersecurity name showing 9% rally with bullish crossover potential as sector rotation gains momentum.

items.name: BlackRock, Inc.
items.note: Asset management giant showing renewed strength with crossover alert after bouncing from bear structure.

items.name: Walt Disney Company (The)
items.note: Media turnaround story gaining traction with bullish EMA setup developing near flatline on 21W.

items.name: Canadian Natural Resources Limited
items.note: Energy leader with orderly low-volume pullback to 13W support — classic quality entry setup.

items.name: Cheniere Energy, Inc.
items.note: LNG export leader benefiting from Iran war disruption with clean low-volume dip buying opportunity.

items.name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
items.note: Oil major showing resilient structure with low-volume pullback amid geopolitical energy premium.

items.name: Deere & Company
items.note: Agricultural equipment leader with orderly pullback — low volume suggests controlled selling pressure.

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic Tech & Storage Leading Acceleration
momentum_themes.detail: Storage and semiconductor names dominating momentum rankings with SNDK up 65% vs 21W leading the charge. Electronic technology sector showing broadest acceleration as AI infrastructure build-out continues.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy vs Tech Divergence Continues

strongest.note: Perfect bullish score with Iran war premium driving all energy names higher.
Industrial renaissance continues with 94% bullish participation and broad momentum.
AI infrastructure theme driving storage/semiconductor acceleration despite mixed signals.

weakest.note: Housing sensitivity weighing on sector with 80% bearish participation.
Software names lagging hardware despite bear rally attempts in select names.
Biotech weakness persisting with regulatory and pricing pressures ongoing.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear energy/industrial outperformance vs tech services/healthcare underperformance. Iran war creating classic commodity rotation while growth tech struggles with valuation reset.

items.text: SPY and QQQ showing compressed EMA spreads with bearish crossover risk despite bullish signals
High volume pullbacks in HWM (1.87x) suggest institutional distribution rather than retail selling
22 Bull Breakdown signals indicate established uptrends starting to crack under pressure
Iran war disruption creating energy spike risks that could pressure broader market if prolonged

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Markets rallied on reports of Iran ceasefire extension hopes, though shipping disruptions continue with seized container ships keeping Strait of Hormuz largely closed. Energy and AI infrastructure names led gains while airlines faced headwinds from fuel cost pressures.

items.text: Iran ceasefire extension hopes boosted risk appetite despite ongoing shipping seizures in Persian Gulf
Energy costs rising across industries from airlines to manufacturing due to supply chain disruptions
LULU CEO change and RIVN production milestone provided individual stock catalysts
AI data center theme continues with mentions of speculative plays and GE Vernova targets raised
Spirit Airlines rescue talks highlight airline sector stress from fuel shock impacts
